title: Electronic device having display with curved edges
abstract: A display may have an array of pixels. The array of pixels may have a shape such as a circular shape or other shape with a curved edge. Display driver circuitry may supply data signals to the pixels using folded vertical data lines and bisected horizontal gate lines. Each folded vertical lines may have a first segment in a left half of the array and a second segment in a right half of the display. Curved coupling segments in an inactive area of the display may be used in joining the first and second segments. Display driver circuits may be provided in top and bottom portions of the inactive area to supply data to respective top and bottom portions of the array. Gate driver output buffers may have different strengths in different rows of the array.
claims:
Claims Claims  What is Claimed is: 1. A display, comprising: an array of pixels forming an active area for the display, wherein the array of pixels has at least one curved edge; an inactive area that surrounds the array of pixels and that has opposing top and bottom portions and opposing left and right portions; and display driver circuitry that includes first display driver circuitry in the top portion; second display driver circuitry in the bottom portion, and gate driver circuitry in the left and right portions. 2. The display defined in claim 1 further comprising first data lines with which the first display driver circuitry supplies a top half of the array of pixels with data and second data lines with which the second display driver circuitry supplies a lower half of the array of pixels with data. 3. The display defined in claim 2 wherein the gate driver circuitry includes a first shift register in the left portion and a second shift register in the right portion. 4. The display defined in claim 3 further comprising control signal lines that run perpendicular to the first and second data lines. 5. The display defined in claim 4 wherein the first and second shift registers each have a chain of registers, wherein each of the registers has an output, and wherein the gate driver circuitry further comprises output drivers coupled between the outputs of the registers and the control signal lines. 6. The display defined in claim 5 wherein the control lines have different lengths in different rows of the array. 7. The display defined in claim 6 wherein at least some of the output drivers have different strengths. 8. The display defined in claim 7 wherein the control lines include at least a first control line and a second control line that is longer than the first control lines, wherein the output drivers include at least a first output driver that supplies the control signals to the first control lines and a second output driver that is stronger than the first output driver and that supplies the control signals to the second control lines. 9. The display defined in claim 1 further comprising gate lines that supply gate line signals from the gate driver circuitry to rows of the pixels, wherein the rows of the pixels have different lengths and wherein the gate driver circuitry includes output drivers of different strengths that supply the gate lines signals to the rows of pixels of different lengths using the gate lines. 10. The display defined in claim 9 wherein the gate lines have different lengths. 11. A display, comprising: an array of pixels forming an active area for the display with at least one curved edge, wherein the active area of the display is surrounded by an inactive area without any pixels and wherein the inactive area has a top portion, a bottom portion, a left portion, and a right portion; and display driver circuitry that includes first circuitry in the top portion that supplies data signals to columns of the pixels in an top portion of the array and that includes second circuitry in the bottom portion that supplies data signals to columns of the pixels in a bottom portion of the display. 12. The display defined in claim 11 wherein the display driver circuitry includes gate driver circuitry that supplies gate lines signals to the pixels over gate lines, wherein the gate driver circuitry includes left gate driver circuitry in the left portion and right 

 gate driver circuitry in the right portion. 13. The display defined in claim 12 wherein the active area is circular. 14. The display defined in claim 13 wherein the inactive area has a circular ring shape that surrounds the active area and wherein the gate driver circuitry lies within the circular ring shape. 15. The display defined in claim 14 wherein the first circuitry includes a first integrated circuit and wherein the second circuitry includes a second integrated circuit. 16. The display defined in claim 15 wherein the gate driver circuitry includes thin-film gate driver circuitry. 17. The display defined in claim 16 wherein the gate driver circuitry has output drivers of different strengths associated respectively with gate lines having different respective lengths. 18. A display, comprising: a circular array of pixels forming a circular active area that displays images, wherein the circular active area is surrounded by an inactive area without any pixels, wherein the inactive area has a top portion, a bottom portion, a left portion, and a right portion, and wherein the circular array of pixels includes a top half and a bottom half; and display driver circuitry that includes first circuitry in the top portion that supplies data signals to columns of the pixels in the top half and that includes second circuitry in the bottom portion that supplies data signals to columns of the pixels in the bottom half. 19. The display defined in claim 18 wherein the display driver circuitry includes gate driver circuitry that supplies gate lines signals to the pixels over gate lines, wherein the gate driver circuitry includes left gate driver circuitry in the left portion and rig 

 gate driver circuitry in the right portion. 20. The display defined in claim 19 wherein the gate driver circuitry has output drivers of different strengths that are associated respectively with gate lines having different respective lengths.
